pitch.title: Grounded
pitch.core_theme: Redemption and finding joy in the simple things.
pitch.logline: When a ruthless alien warlord crashes on Earth, his plans for world domination are derailed when he develops an intense, intoxicating addiction to human coffee, forcing him to protect the planet from his own armada to save his favorite diner.
pitch.synopsis:
Act 1: Zorgon the Annihilator crash-lands near a sleepy Pacific Northwest town. Preparing a beacon for his invasion fleet, he tries a cup of black coffee at a local diner and experiences a euphoric awakening.

Act 2: Zorgon becomes a regular, befriending the struggling diner owner, Sarah. When his armada finally arrives to harvest Earth's resources, Zorgon realizes they will destroy the coffee bean crops. He uses his advanced weaponry to fight off his own scouting parties, disguised as a quirky government agent.

Act 3: An all-out battle ensues. Zorgon rallies the townspeople, weaponizing espresso machines and caffeine-fueled tactics to defeat the armada commander. He chooses to stay on Earth as the diner's new barista, protecting his beloved brew.

stage2_outline.title: Grounded
stage2_outline.logline: When a ruthless alien warlord crashes on Earth, his plans for world domination are derailed when he develops an intense, intoxicating addiction to human coffee, forcing him to protect the planet from his own armada to save his favorite diner.

beats.description: Zorgon the Annihilator stands on the bridge of his warship, watching a planet burn, establishing his ruthless nature.
During a routine jump, Zorgon's flagship suffers a catastrophic malfunction, forcing him into an escape pod that crashes into the woods outside a sleepy Pacific Northwest town.
Zorgon emerges from the wreckage, activates a distress beacon set to summon his armada in seventy-two hours, and prepares to subjugate the local human populace.
Adopting a flimsy, awkward human disguise, Zorgon ventures into town to gather intelligence and assess the planet's defenses.
Exhausted by Earth's gravity, he enters a struggling local diner owned by Sarah and demands her strongest liquid sustenance.

Sarah serves him a simple cup of black coffee. Zorgon takes a sip and experiences a mind-bending, euphoric awakening. Intoxicated by the brew, he secretly pauses the invasion beacon's countdown to ensure the diner remains intact.
Zorgon becomes a daily customer at the diner, obsessing over different roasts, grinds, and pour techniques while hiding his alien nature.
He befriends Sarah, learning about her financial struggles to keep the diner open, and begins to appreciate the simple joys of human life.

A minor alien scout ship arrives to investigate the delayed beacon. Zorgon hacks their database and discovers their protocol is to glass the planet's surface, which would instantly incinerate all global coffee plantations.
Desperate to protect the coffee supply, Zorgon creates a fake persona as a quirky government agent to intercept his own scouts.
Zorgon actively engages and destroys the scout ship to protect an incoming shipment of rare Ethiopian beans, officially committing treason against his own empire to save his addiction.
Zorgon juggles his double life, defending the town from escalating scout incursions while helping Sarah repair and upgrade the diner's failing equipment.
The alien armada grows suspicious of the missing scouts and accelerates their arrival, sending elite trackers to hunt down the traitor.
During a skirmish behind the diner, Sarah discovers Zorgon's glowing alien weaponry and confronts him, demanding the truth.
Zorgon confesses his true identity and his original plan to subjugate humanity, breaking Sarah's trust and alienating his only friend.
The Armada Commander, Zorgon's ruthless rival, lands his dreadnought in the town center and blasts the diner's storefront to rubble.
Zorgon is captured, stripped of his warlord rank, and forced to kneel as the Commander orders the immediate orbital bombardment of Earth's agricultural centers.
Sarah and the townspeople, realizing Zorgon sacrificed everything for them, use his hidden alien tech to break him out of the Commander's holding cell.
Zorgon reveals that the alien energy shields are highly vulnerable to super-heated, pressurized liquids.
The townspeople rally together, weaponizing commercial espresso machines and milk steamers to create a heavily caffeinated, makeshift militia ready to defend their home.
Zorgon leads the charge, using caffeine-fueled combat tactics to dismantle the Armada's ground forces and ultimately defeating the Commander in a brutal hand-to-hand duel.
Zorgon officially cancels the invasion, sending the fleet back into deep space, and renounces his title as the Annihilator.
Zorgon, wearing a green apron over his alien armor, expertly pulls a perfect shot of espresso at the newly rebuilt diner, finally at peace.
